free_text: Simulated (n,f) cross sections for exotic actinide
nuclei
Charged particle beams were provided
by the Los Alamos tandem and three-stage Van de Graaff
facility
Standard Si E-dE telescope was used for
charged particle measurement. Detectors were 300 mu-m
and 2mm thick silicon surface barrier detectors.
Fission detector placed at forward angle is not
described.
Coincidence between charged particle and
fission fragments was required for determination of
fission probabilities.
Surrogate method was used to measure fission
cross sections of exotic actinides. Cross section ratio
for reactions (3He,df)/(3He,d) and (3He,tf)/(3He,t)
were measured and respective fission probabilities
determined. Assuming approximately constant
compound nucleus cross section and similar spin
distribution in compound nuclei, the "simulated"
(n,f) cross sections were determined as a product of
measured fission probability and compound nucleus
formation cross section.
The normalization of the simulated
cross section is believed to be accurate to
approximately 10% at ~1 MeV, while in the region from
0.5 to 1 MeV, systematic errors on the order of 15. to
20% are possible.
Statistical uncertainties are given on several figs.
Compiled by S.H.
SD: SF1 and DECAY-DATA were
corrected in Sub.6.
OS. DECAY-DATA in sub.25 corrected.
